quote_of_day.text:
>>11031
Old Gen overpriced jets

Madarchod don't care about country, your state , your people , your caste, your relatives. none of that shit matters btw. you should only focus on yourself. you're just surviving in this country. rest of the things are just optional
quote_of_day.name: Anon

articles.title: Weather on the board today
articles.body:
A low-pressure system of government overreach moved into the board this morning, as Anon discussed the Lundia government's crackdown on doctors criticizing Ayurveda. The system brought a flurry of comments, with one anon calling it an 'absolute fucking state of Pajeetland'.

A scattered high-pressure ridge of social commentary formed later in the day, with Anon debating whether tier 1 city women are adopting 'slutty' clothing. This system was fueled by comparisons to Reddit fashion subs and garnered a significant number of responses.

Meanwhile, a gentle drizzle of historical discussion fell across the board, as Anon examined caste roles in ancient Indian military.

Additionally, two smaller disturbances - a cousin's rishta rejection and a list of Modi's 'achievements' - sparked brief but notable discussions, contributing to an overcast atmosphere.

articles.title: Lundia Govt Targets Docs Criticising Ayurveda
articles.body:
The government of Lundia is going after social media accounts of doctors who criticise Ayush and Ayurveda/Homeopathy, calling them pseudoscience.

This move has sparked concerns among users, who see it as an attempt to silence critics of the government's favoured traditional medicine systems.

One user pointed out that a government-funded Ayurvedic hospital was recently exposed by a doctor on social media, and now the government is targeting him.

The hospital in question, CNS Ayurveda Hospital, has been accused of treating children for chronic conditions such as severe mental health disorders.

Some users defended Ayurveda, saying it has useful medicines and supplements that could be studied and researched for aid in medicine. However, many others were scathing in their criticism, calling Ayurveda a "scam" and "fakemaxxing".

They argued that the system is unregulated and has killed people with its medicines.

The conversation also touched on the use of WhatsApp by scientists to share sensitive information, with one user joking that this was evidence of the lack of development in Lundia.

Others criticised the government for using AI to generate propaganda, and for not taking action on racism against Indians on social media.

The thread also saw users sharing their own experiences with Ayurvedic products, including one who found mercury in their mother's Ayurveda foot cream and another who used Patanjali Gau Mutra soap.

The government's move has been seen as an attempt to suppress criticism of Ayurveda and other traditional medicine systems, and to promote them as viable alternatives to modern medicine.

articles.title: Anon Questions if Tier 1 City Women Are Adopting 'Slutty' Clothing
articles.body:
Anon opened a discussion on whether women in tier 1 cities have started dressing in a 'slutty' style, similar to what is seen on Reddit's fashion subs.

They wondered if this is a genuine trend or an attempt by 'da jooz' to desensitize Indian men to raunchy dressing.

One respondent disagreed that normal women in tier 1 cities dress this way, suggesting that Reddit is becoming popular in India and 'jooz' are using it for psychological operations.

Another anon claimed that Jewish people have brainwashed women into dressing provocatively through media like movies and TV shows, and that women enjoy getting attention.

Anon countered that they have become desensitized to cleavage and revealing clothing, blaming 'jews' for making this type of dress seem normal.

Some anons discussed the idea that 'tradwives' do not exist in 2026, even in North India, and that dalit women now follow 'pheminijam'.

One respondent contradicted the claim that it's rare to see normal women wearing such clothes, stating that it is normal in cities like Delhi, Bangalore, Chennai, Hyderabad, and Mumbai.

Anon also accused 'jooz' of using AI to post content and create the illusion of more randis on social media.

The discussion took a turn with an anon sharing a personal experience of visiting Chennai to see women dressed in revealing clothing, and another jokingly asking if someone was being desensitized by the 'smell of cow dung'.

The thread also included some vulgar and off-topic comments.

articles.title: Caste Roles in Ancient Indian Military
articles.body:
Anon posted a list of caste-based roles in an ancient Indian military, including foot soldiers as shuddhars and tribals, officers as rajus, and defence experts as brahmin scholars.

One anon countered that rajus were not ordinary foot soldiers but cavaliers and commanders, and that shuddhars made up the foot soldiers, led by their commanders. Another anon interjected that the caste system was fluid, with dysgenics ending up in undesirable jobs.

Anon responded with claims of historical accuracy, stating that rajputs were cavaliers and commanders, not foot soldiers. The discussion took a turn with personal attacks, with one anon telling another their ancestors were only fit for cleaning.

Comparisons were drawn to modern-day Ghazni, with one anon describing it as having clean air, food, and water, and being populated by honest and healthy people.

The topic of jizya tax also came up, with one anon noting that their ancestors did not have to pay it. The thread included several instances of 'KYS' (likely 'Know Your Shit'), with one anon telling another to study history.

articles.title: Cousin's rishta rejection sparks debate
articles.body:
Anon shared a story about their landwhale cousin who rejected a rishta with an IAS officer due to his height. The cousin also slapped a drunk cousin at a wedding for abusing her parents.

One respondent praised the cousin, calling her 'based' and suggesting she would be more attractive if she lost weight for health reasons. Others discussed the merits of self-acceptance versus health improvements.

Replies ranged from supporting the cousin's choices to making derogatory comments about landwhales.

articles.title: Anon Lists Modi's 'Achievements'
articles.body:
Anon started a thread asking for major achievements of India's longest-serving prime minister. Anon listed several 'achievements': having a world tour, buying chocolates for a person named Melody, and religious politics in northern India.

They also mentioned Operation Sindoor and the Ram Mandir. Another anon claimed Modi's focus on welfare schemes is a scam and that he prioritizes neither education nor R&D.

Anon also brought up India's relationship with Pakistan, with one suggesting Modi is controlled by his 'Jew masters'.
